evidence_strength: committed implementation and deterministic tests; runtime execution not observed in this connector run

primary_sources.relevance: Identifier_Status filtering, Default_Ignorable treatment, and confusable detection are separate mechanisms that must be composed without overstating conformance.
Unicode identifier syntax is a separate acceptance layer; this slice does not claim XID or full profile conformance.
Default_Ignorable_Code_Point data must match the authenticated IdentifierStatus and confusables version.

finding: An authenticated Unicode bundle is not operationally useful until the exact verified bytes are the only bytes admitted to the Identifier_Status evaluator, Default_Ignorable predicate, and confusable skeleton registry. A single fail-closed constructor can preserve that chain and return one admission decision with separate profile, collision, provenance, and trust-limit fields.
design_implication: Identifier registration should be constructed from one exact-version digest-verified bundle and should reject when profile eligibility or skeleton uniqueness fails. Authentication, eligibility, uniqueness, and malicious intent remain distinct claims.

observed: Both artifacts were committed to the default branch.
The test contract covers authenticated construction, Restricted rejection, confusable collision rejection, Default_Ignorable parsing, digest drift rejection, and overlapping-range rejection.

falsification_route: Run node --test tools/frontier-research/unicode-identifier-admission.test.mjs and retain exact output.
Run the gate over the full pinned Unicode 17.0.0 files and compare skeleton/reference cases against UTS #39.
Demonstrate any digest-mismatched bundle, Restricted scalar, or duplicate skeleton that is accepted.
Demonstrate a Default_Ignorable_Code_Point that remains in the computed internal skeleton.
limits: No cryptographic proof that manifest digests came from a trusted channel.
No inference of malicious intent from confusability.
privacy_boundary: Public standards, repository paths, abstract test identifiers, and commit IDs only; no private chat, health, credentials, or identifying personal material.
